- batch-fetch-traders: add per-platform timeout scaled to group size to prevent
  one stuck platform from blocking the entire batch within 300s Vercel limit

fix: ideas not saving to DB — asyncpg requires datetime not str

The created_at was passed as an ISO string (.isoformat()) but asyncpg
requires a datetime object for TIMESTAMPTZ columns. This caused all
_save_idea_to_db calls to fail silently (return_exceptions=True).

Also:
- Added error logging for save/push failures so they surface in logs
- Updated _format_idea_markdown and _push_idea_to_github to handle
  datetime objects (strftime instead of string slicing)
- Fixed "Papers With Code" label in IdeasClient hero → HuggingFace

commit.message:
perf: limit RSS items to 25 per feed to reduce scoring overhead

Google News RSS feeds were returning 100+ articles per sector, resulting in
979+ candidates. Corroboration scoring is O(n²), so 979 articles required
~958k comparisons, causing 4+ minute cron runtimes.

Limiting to 25 most recent items per feed reduces candidates to ~250,
cutting scoring time from minutes to seconds while keeping high-quality
recent articles.

This maintains coverage of recent news while preventing excessive scoring
overhead in the validation cron.

commit.message:
refactor(tests): replace fixed waits with dynamic event polling in SuggestionManager tests (#275)

* refactor(tests): replace fixed waits with dynamic event polling in SuggestionManager tests

Replace brittle `await wait(Xms)` calls with `waitForNextCall()` and `waitFor()` helpers
that poll for observable state changes instead of sleeping a fixed duration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve lint and format issues in SuggestionManager test helpers

Add curly braces to single-line if statements (eslint curly rule) and
auto-format with prettier. Also exclude .claude/ from prettier checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(tests): replace all remaining fixed waits with fake timers

Add withFakeTimers() helper that wraps jest.useFakeTimers() +
advanceTimersByTime() + useRealTimers() for negative assertion tests
(asserting getPrediction is NOT called). Remove the now-unused wait()
helper. Suite runs in ~6.8s vs ~9.6s before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: cancel pending restart timer on disable to prevent stale re-enables

When `restart()` schedules a `setTimeout(..., 0)`, calling `disable()` (e.g.
via `destroy()`) did not cancel that timer. The leaked timer could fire during
the next test's async module import, inserting a stale SuggestionManager
instance into the shared harness and causing intermittent test failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix tool calls appearing at top of chat after session switch

When returning to a session with an active task, rebindSocket replays
tool_start/tool_end events before the async message history loads.
The replayed tool calls got lower seq numbers than the subsequently
loaded messages, causing them to sort at the top of the timeline.

Re-sequence activeToolCalls after loading history so they sort after
the persisted messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add stealth browser fallback for CF-protected exchanges

Pionex, Crypto.com, and LBank are behind Cloudflare JS Challenge
which VPS proxy alone can't bypass. Added 3-tier fallback chain:
1. Direct HTTP → 2. VPS proxy → 3. Stealth browser (Playwright)

Also added these domains to CF Worker and VPS proxy whitelists.
VPS proxy on SG (45.76.152.169) restarted with new whitelist.

commit.message:
fix(wordpress): transliterate filename to ASCII for Content-Disposition

Non-ASCII characters in article titles (e.g. Romanian ș, ă) caused
UnicodeEncodeError in the Content-Disposition header. Now uses NFKD
normalization + ASCII encoding to transliterate (e.g. "ș" → "s").

commit.message:
Fix reward builder sliders not updating chart

Canvas sizing used offsetWidth which can return 0 before layout
resolves, silently setting the canvas to 0×0 and preventing all draws.
Replaced with getBoundingClientRect() + zero-size guard. Replaced
fragile setTimeout(50) init with requestAnimationFrame. Tightened
slider wiring into a single declarative loop.

commit.message:
feat: ROADMAP 3.0 Group 7 — Stripe Integration & Buy Me a Coffee

Backend:
- Add Stripe subscription fields to User model (stripeCustomerId,
  subscriptionStatus, subscriptionTier, trialEndsAt, currentPeriodEnd)
- Add StripeWebhookEvent model for idempotent webhook logging
- stripeService: lazy Stripe init, checkout sessions (7-day trial),
  customer portal, subscriptionToUserData mapper
- stripeWebhookHandler: handles subscription.updated/deleted,
  invoice.payment_failed/succeeded, checkout.session.completed
- requirePremium middleware: 402 + PREMIUM_REQUIRED for free users
- Routes: GET /api/stripe/subscription, POST /checkout, /portal, /webhook
- Wire Stripe customer creation fire-and-forget on user register
- Add paymentFailedTemplate + sendPaymentFailed to email service
- Fix JWT expiry response: 403 → 401 with user-friendly message
- Add Alert.alert before auto-logout on session expiry

Frontend:
- useSubscription hook: fetch status, startCheckout, openPortal,
  trialDaysLeft computed from trialEndsAt
- PaywallScreen: monthly/annual toggle, feature list, trial badge,
  manage subscription portal link
- PremiumGate: wraps premium-only UI with upgrade CTA for free users
- CoffeeBanner: 7-day AsyncStorage cooldown, triggers after cooking
  complete, never shown to Premium/trialing users
- Profile: "Support Sazon ☕" row for free-tier users
- paywall.tsx: Expo Router modal screen
- stripeApi group in lib/api.ts

Tests (19 backend + 17 frontend):
- stripe.test.ts: subscriptionToUserData, getOrCreateCustomer,
  requirePremium (6 cases), webhook handler (7 cases)
- useSubscription.test.ts: fetch states, trialDaysLeft, API calls
- CoffeeBanner.test.tsx: render, premium gate, dismiss,
  shouldShowCoffeeBanner cooldown, recordCoffeeBannerShown

commit.message:
Fix duplicate inline images in chat when Pi creates a plot

Chokidar fires both add and change events for new files, which can
land in separate debounce windows. addPendingImages now deduplicates
against already-pending paths to prevent the same image appearing twice.

commit.message:
Fix CSP blocking inline onclick handlers in production

Add script-src-attr 'unsafe-inline' directive to Helmet CSP config.
CSP Level 3 requires this separate directive for inline event handlers
(onclick, onsubmit, etc.) — script-src alone only covers <script> tags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sign-in button: replace inline onclick with addEventListener

The inline onclick handler for the sign-in dropdown may be blocked
by Content Security Policy in production. Switch to addEventListener
for CSP compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(wordpress): upload-media now sets featured image on published post

When uploading an image via the separate upload-media endpoint, if the
image belongs to an article that's already published to WordPress AND
is the article's featured image, automatically attach it to the post
and set it as featured_media. Also generates SEO metadata from article
context for the upload.

This fixes the flow where user publishes first, generates image second,
then uploads to WP — previously the image was unattached in the library.
